data.current_description:
Files smaller than 5GB can be uploaded as a single part upload in one shot.

But files bigger than 5 GB must be uploaded in multiple parts/chunks where each chunk size should be at least 5 MB, except the last chunk.The user can specify the parts/chunks in the request body.

Note that the file can be less than 5MB for a single part upload.

**Upload Steps:**

1) First, use this POST API to initiate the file upload and it will return a list of pre-signed URL(s), along with fileId and id, that can to be used to upload each part/chunk of the file directly to S3.The length of the list of pre signed urls in the response will be equal to the number of parts/chunks that is passed in the request body.By default, if parts is not passed in the request body, the API sets the parts/chunks to 1 which will be then same as a single part upload.

2) Use the pre signed url(s) from the response in step 1 above to upload the file directly to AWS S3 using a PUT request. The file has to be supplied in the request body as binary file. Depending on the connection bandwidth this AWS S3 API call may take a while for very large files. Either you wait until the call is successful OR you can upload the file in smaller chunks. Save the value(s) in the ETag response header and pass that in the Parts array in the next step.

3) Finally, PUT Finalize File upload(>10MB) API must be called using the fileId and id returned from this API in step 1 and Etag from step 2.
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate multipart file upload and retrieve pre-signed URLs
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a multipart file upload workflow for files larger than 10 MB (up to 5 GB). Returns a list of pre-signed URLs for direct S3 upload, along with fileId and id required for finalization. Each part must be at least 5 MB except the last chunk. Specify the number of parts in the request body; defaults to 1 for single-part uploads. After uploading each part to S3, capture the ETag header values and pass them to the CompleteMultipartUpload endpoint to finalize the upload.
